Character ID
Companion_Caroc
Guid
b1a7e802-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
The
Devil of Caroc
is a female
construct
rogue
and potential
companion
in
The White March - Part I
.
Background
The Devil of Caroc is a bronze golem inhabited by the soul of a convicted murderer. She's ruthless and relentless, yet something about the player piques her interest when they first meet.
The Devil was once a trapper in
Cold Morn
, a village that became notorious for its role in the
Saint's War
. When she and her neighbors let the
Readceran
troops pass through town unopposed, they became a symbol of treachery and cowardice throughout
Dyrwood
. The Purges that swept the country began in Cold Morn and the Devil's family perished when an angry mob burned the village to the ground. The Devil escaped, suffering severe burns that left her scarred. Afterwards, she began hunting down those who participated in the purge of her village, committing over a dozen murders by burning them alive in their own homes, starting with the small village of
Caroc
. Her hunt eventually led her to
Stalwart Village
in 2813 AI, where she was eventually apprehended and locked away to await execution.
However, a Vailian Animancer named
Galvino
bribed the mayor so that he could use the Devil in his experiments. This saved the Devil from a stoning death, but condemned her to an existence trapped inside an unfeeling bronze body. Worse, she became an outcast together with Galvino when the people of Stalwart learned of the deal and ran them all out of town, destroying Galvino's lab and equipment in the process. The Devil of Caroc ended up staying with Galvino out of necessity; he was the only person capable of the maintenance her body required to keep her functioning, plus she'd rather not succumb to the elements or the hunting parties from Stalwart.
The years passing by have not eroded her, however: She's still a rustic at heart, but her crude manners belie cunning and a cynical sense of humor.
Interactions
This character is
involved in
quests
.
Lair of the Eyeless
The One that Got Away
Companion
The Devil of Caroc becomes available when you complete
the Recluse of the White March
and requires either paying
Galvino
or using
Perception
12 to play on the
animancer
's pride to gain her. Another choice is to kill Galvino, after which you can talk directly to her.
Quests
The One That Got Away
: The Devil of Caroc's companion quest starts when you visit
Stalwart Village
with her in the party and talk to her.
Lair of the Eyeless
: The Devil doesn't need to breathe and doesn't care about the cold, making her the perfect choice to strike the crystal.

Initial points
Devil of Caroc's initial
skill
,
talent
, and
ability
distribution depends on the level of the Watcher when she is first added to the party, but only if the "Auto Level Companions" option is checked in the difficulty settings.
With auto-levelling enabled, companions will be levelled following a preset point distribution. The player is still free to distribute points into whatever skills, abilities, and talents they desire when a companion levels up, and they may be completely retrained at an
inn
. Devil of Caroc always starts at a minimum of level 2 (the stats above are before she joins the party).
With auto-levelling disabled, companions are not levelled, and the player may choose their own point distribution. Devil of Caroc starts at level 1 with the requisite experience to level her up to the current player level or level 2, whichever is higher.

Character ID
NPC_BACKER_Doran_Chamberlain
Guid
d4d38a02-f890-45da-8699-f0c6c7f84e6f
Doran Chamberlain
is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found in
Gilded Vale
, near the
home
on the northern edge of the area.
Background
As you near, you feel a vibrant history contained in the essence of this man's soul. Voices from its past seem to call out to you.
You see a large crowd gathered in the middle of a courtyard. This man stands in the midst of them, a cloak pulled over his head, holding it closed around his neck. The crowd has congregated around a platform and they all watch, transfixed, as a man is led to the platform, flanked by two city guardsmen. The man is shackled hand and foot but carries himself as a nobleman, not deigning to even glance at the crowd.
The cloaked man pulls his cover closer around his face and turns to leave, hunching over to avoid being seen. A crier on the platform unrolls a parchment and begins to read, but the man pays no attention, intent on getting away from the courtyard. The crier's voice grows louder and more intense as he reads and voice from the crowd start to punctuate his sentences. The jeers and shouts grow more frequent and with each one, the cloaked man flinches and his shoulders droop. He breaks from the crowd, coming to the edge of the courtyard as the crier reaches the end of his proclamation. The crowd grows silent again save for the occasional shout. The man turns back to look at the platform, seeing his father kneeling before the block, neck exposed.
The executioner moves into position, lifting his axe to strike. The man turns suddenly, unable to watch. There is a wet thunk and the crowd breaks into cheers and applause. He leans against the wall, regaining his composure, then straightens up. With obvious resolve, he strides off, away from the crowd and his legacy.
Interactions
This character is a
backer-created NPC
.

0b9c4f92-09fa-47e7-b515-25208a70c47c
“
Outwardly he prefers to come off a bit of a simpleton. Don't be fooled. Before he was a duc he was a trapper, and an excellent one at that.
”
~
Lady
Eydis Webb
Duc Aevar Wolf-Grin
is the ruler of
Dyrwood
in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be encountered in the
Ducal Palace
in the
First Fires
district of
Defiance Bay
, during
The Hermit of Hadret House
.
Background
A wild looking man with a scraggly beard, Aevar Wolf-Grin is a man of animal cunning and unmatched political aptitude.
A frontier noble elected to the position of duc of the Free Palatinate, Duc Aevar is a shrewd politician and excellent judge of character. He has deliberately cultivated a specific image over the years through half-truths and exaggerations, allowing him to be many things to a great many people. To the frontiersmen, he is one of their own. To the patriotic, he is a soul descendant of Admeth Hadret. To the nobility, he is from an old and established family.
Though he is none of these in full, it was enough to grant him the throne of the Free Palatinate and title of duc. The man  may not be the greatest mind in Dyrwood, but he knows people. He knows when they are sincere and when they lie. He knows when they are strong and when they are weak. Whenever he needs advice on other matters, he relies on Lady
Eydis Webb
and the
Dunryd Row
.
Interactions
This character is
involved in
quests
.
The Hermit of Hadret House
Quests
The Hermit of Hadret House
: Your only interaction with him is during the extended cut-scene/dialog when you attend the
animancy hearings
.

Character ID
NPC_BACKER_Dueggan_Steelcaller
Guid
ca4d6e9c-4c88-4bfc-92c8-772e680f339c
Dueggan Steelcaller
is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found in the
Black Hammer Smithery
, on the eastern side of
Gilded Vale
Background
As you near, you feel a vibrant history contained in the essence of this man's soul. Voices from its past seem to call out to you.
You see a group of adventurers surrounded by a crowd of attacking xaurips. This man is with them, seemingly unconcerned by the battle, even detached from it as he stands in the middle of his comrades, chanting, his expression bordering on elation. His voice is powerful and deep, booming out across the combatants as a rich counterpoint to the chaos of their fighting.
With every phrase, the battlefield changes. His allies glow with a pulsing blue light, freezing the xaurips that surround them. A blazing pyre erupts from the ground, cutting and burning the creatures and sending them scattering. One of the fallen xaurips suddenly explodes as three giant grubs crawl from its corpse and attack the remaining enemies.
The man continues to chant, thoroughly enthralled in the joy of the moment. Finally, the creatures have been reduced to one. His allies stand back as the man approaches it, still chanting. He removes his hammer and swings it at the xaurip. His phrase ends as the hammer connects with its head, punctuating the spell and bringing his song to an end.
Interactions
This character is a
backer-created NPC
.

Durance
Biography and appearance
Game

Gameplay

Durance's Staff

2
Rod
Technical
Character ID
Companion_GGP
Guid
b1a7e801-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
“
It's what's beneath the skin and the letters I care about, what burns within. It's more important to me you're a Watcher than whatever culture or accent decorates your letters like awkward crowns.
Take pride in your actions, not where you hail from... Or how your name rolls off whatever liar's tongue coats it now.
”
Durance
is a male
human
priest
and one of the recruitable
companions
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
He can be found in
Magran's Fork
, at the shrine of Magran  in the center of the map. You simply need to talk to him, and unless refused, he will join the party. Player characters who are affiliated with Magran will have additional dialogue options.
Background
Durance is an incredibly ugly man, with bulging red cheeks, raked by pox scars and a scraggly beard. He appears to be in his early 40s and wears the frayed robes of
Magran
. He wields a
unique staff
that has a magical flame.
Durance is not his name
and it is never revealed.
Beliefs
Durance studied Magranic scripture at
Ashfall
. However, he is part of a non-popular sect that observes the teachings of Durance.
Durance (the original) sought insight from meditation while watching a flame. If that failed, he would seek conflict instead.
His teachings were comprised of trials and lessons and his followers were usually not welcomed because they were often hostile to other Magran clergy they viewed as weak. His followers saw this outcast status as a trial and their wandering nature became a sign of "physical devotion".
A point of contention between Durance followers and the rest of the Magranic order was their views on justice. Traditionally,
Woedica
is the god of justice. However, Durance's followers believed in dispensing "justice" during their wandering trials. They used Woedica's exile as justification that their actions were appropriate for followers of Magran.
Saint's War
Durance was part of a team of twelve priests from Ashfall who worked on the
Godhammer Bomb
that ended the
Saint's War
at
Halgot Citadel
.
Each member of the team was awarded a staff made from the Ashfall forest that was blackened during the
War of Black Trees
.
Durance was the only priest that survived the blast. However, his body was left scarred and his soul was damaged.
Aftermath and Purges
Durance found himself shaken after the events at Halgot Citadel. He felt Magran turned her back to him and fell silent.
At first he headed back to Ashfall, but changed course after deciding to follow the teachings of Durance.
He joined
the Purges
, killing many Eothasians. He recalls this as "saving the Eothasians from themselves. Burning any that didn't die with their god."
When
Waidwen's Legacy
struck, Durance felt it appropriate to kill any
Hollowborn
he crossed paths with.
Conflicts
As a fiercely loyal citizen of the
Free Palatinate of Dyrwood
and a zealous follower of
Magran
, Durance is vocal about being at odds with many groups:
Eothas
and his followers, Eothasians, because of
Waidwen
.
Hylea
and her followers, whom sheltered Eothasians after the
Saint's War
.
The nation of
Readceras
, where Waidwen was from.
The
Aedyr Empire
that once ruled the Dyrwood.
Animancy
,
ciphers
and
watchers
. All of which he feels contirbuted to Waidwen's Legacy.
Orlans
.
Pillars of Eternity
In the Watcher, Durance sees a person who can make the barren field of the Dyrwood fertile again and latches on to test them, to ensure that they speak truth, not lies, but the learning goes both ways. Over time he comes to learn that the gods are not unlike the authority ﬁgures he so often ﬁnds himself at odds with, and that he and the killed Magranic clergy were perhaps nothing but pieces in the gods’ games (see
endings
).
Interactions
Pillars of Eternity
This character is a
party member
.
This character
starts
quests
.
The Trials of Durance
Companion
Durance is found at
Magran's Fork
towards the center of the map by a shrine to Magran. Note that he only appears after speaking to the dead dwarf
Caldara de Berranzi
in
Gilded Vale
during
Visions and Whispers
.
He can provide support and healing as a
priest
.
He also starts with points in
mechanics
, making him a good companion to tackle locks and traps.
While not particularly conversational with anyone other than the Watcher, his personality is confrontational and consequently most of his
banter
is argumentative with other companions.
Durance keeps secrets that unravel the more you talk to him. He will tire of talking and periodically stops answering until after resting.
Companion quest
Durance's companion quest is
The Trials of Durance
, which is received as soon as you recruit him.
His quest is progressed after resting with him in the party and seeing a vision.
A second vision will become available after resting with him in the party in Act 3.
The quest can be completed after
Council of Stars
in Act 3.
Endings
Using knowledge gleaned from his dialogue, you can try to convince Durance that Magran conspired with Woedica, and that she no longer speaks to Durance not because he did something to offend her, but because the damage to his soul has left Magran blind to his being.
This has the effect of making Durance realize that the violence and brutality he perpetrated on Eothasians - and, perhaps, the Godhammer - to regain her favor were unnecessary. If he comes to the conclusion that Magran did, in fact, try to kill all the makers of the Godhammer at Halgot Citadel, and he only escaped with the wounds his soul sustained in the blast by sheer luck, he will swear revenge at Magran, promising that he will continue to use and drain her power as long as he needs, until he finally finds a way to strike back at her.
Failing to convince Durance of this plot results in his belief that Magran's continued silence is condemnation of his existence.
Other interactions
If he is an active member of the party when finding the
burning stone
during
Cinders of Faith
, Durance has extra dialogue in which he refers to the stone as "a whore's cruel jest" and a "divine practical joke." He refuses to explain the joke, as he considers himself the only one who would get it. But potentially, the burning stone can be regarded as a metaphor for Durance himself: another cast-off, broken, remainder of the
Godhammer
's explosion.
If he is in the party at
Teir Evron
during
Council of Stars
, Durance has extra dialog in which he tries to gets Magran's attention, but she ignores him.
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
This character has
other interactions
.
Minor references
If recruited in the first game,
Xoti
will see Durance in her dreams as a skeletal priest.
When speaking with
Eld Engrim
after the prologue you can refer to Durance.
If you did convince him of Magran and Woedica's plot:
Watcher:
"The last Magranite to travel with me almost went mad because of his goddess' trials."
Engrim:
"Aye, but almost mad ain't fully mad. Ah, Old Durance. The goddess shan't forge another like him in our lives, captain."
If you didn't convince him of the plot:
Watcher:
"The last Magranite to travel with me wasn't quite up to the goddess' challenge."
Engrim:
"Aye. Poor Durance. May the mad whoreson see better days come the next turn of the Wheel."
Magran
references her plot with Woedica after leaving
Port Maje
:
"I acted in haste during the Saint's War. You will not goad me into doing the same now."
Edér
references Durance's ramblings during his personal quest
The Lighted Path
when dealing with the
Partisans of the Lighted Path
:
"You almost wish you could lock 'em in a room with Durance. See how fast they talk their way through the air supply."

Initial points
Durance's initial
skill
,
talent
, and
ability
distribution depends on the level of the Watcher when he is first added to the party, but only if the "Auto Level Companions" option is checked in the difficulty settings.
With auto-levelling enabled, companions will be levelled following a preset point distribution. The player is still free to distribute points into whatever skills, abilities, and talents they desire when a companion levels up, and they may be completely retrained at an
inn
. Durance always starts at a minimum of level 2 (the stats above are before he joins the party).
With auto-levelling disabled, companions are not levelled, and the player may choose their own point distribution. Durance starts at level 1 with the requisite experience to level him up to the current player level or level 2, whichever is higher.

Behind the scenes
Durance was written by
Chris Avellone
.
Before PoE 1.03, Durance had the following Attributes: Mig: 14 Con: 15 Dex: 9 Per: 9 Int: 13 Res: 19.
Durance is voiced by actor
Dave B. Mitchell
, who is also the narrator.
A conflict in their past was planned for Durance and the
Grieving Mother
, and may have been resolved during the story of
the Watcher
, but that didn't make it into the release version of the
game
.
Durance's name in the game files is "companion_GGP". This is also the name used in console commands.

Dying Monk
is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found on the eastern side of the creek in northeastern corner of
Elmshore
.
Background
The man on the ground gasps for air, clutching his chest as he struggles. On closer examination, you see his red cloak obscures the full extent of his injuries. His face whitens as blood pools around him. "Please. Traveler..."
He reaches out, arduously extending his hand to you.
This dying monk was carrying a message to his order in
Elms' Reach
, when he was ambushed by a pack of wild stelgaers. Cuts on his knuckles from the morning's practise drew the beasts, which had smelled the fresh blood in the air. He put up a fight but was fatally wounded in the process. Unable to finish his mission himself, he entrusts the Watcher to carry the message on its final leg.
Interactions
This character
starts
quests
.
The Sealed Missive
This character has
other interactions
.
With
Honest
,
Cruel
,
Benevolent
, or
Deceptive
disposition 2, the monk will recognize you as the Watcher and will know of your deeds.
As a
Monk
, you can suggest he try to transcend the pain and draw strength from his injuries.
Quests
The Sealed Missive
: In his dying breaths he gives you a
scroll
to deliver to his brothers at the
Celestial Sapling
in
Hearthsong
.

“

”
Eadric Morley
is a delegate of the
Dozens
in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be encountered in the
Ducal Palace
in the
First Fires
district of
Defiance Bay
during
The Hermit of Hadret House
.
Background
Coming from the people, Eadric Morley has lived in poverty his entire life and joined the Dozens to make an impact on the world. His life has been defined by the birth of two Hollowborn children, a son and a daughter. Both of them passed, one drowning in bath, the other from respiratory failure in the night. As Morley's family had no money to bury them properly, they were interred beneath the floorboards of their house.
Bitter and resentful, Eadric blamed animancers for exploiting the crisis - and likely causing it. As such, he was a natural choice to represent the
Dozens
at the
animancy hearings
held by the
duc
.

This character is
involved in
quests
.
The Hermit of Hadret House
Quests

: Your only interaction with him is during the extended cut-scene/dialog when you attend the
animancy hearings
.

9ee4446f-160c-43e0-a1c8-ed535d6909d1
Edlyn Preostun
is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
. She can be found in the
home
on the northern side of
Gilded Vale
.
Background
As you near, you feel a vibrant history contained in the essence of this woman's soul. Voices from its past seem to call out to you.
You see a key turned gently, steady, and hear a ghostly click as the door swings inward. Two figures slip in, locking the door behind them, and begin their prowl. Their hunt is efficient, and it takes them little time to acquire a sizable collection of documents and oddly shaped velvet pouches. Quick hands fill pockets, rucksacks, bags.
The door opens downstairs and everything freezes. Footsteps. A tired giggle. The thieves are all eye-whites and short breath as they empty their pockets of all but the necessities - anything noisy hurriedly replaced. Silence from downstairs, then tentative steps up the staircase. A voice calls out. One thief creeps forward, the other gesticulating wildly for him to return. He continues forward, crouching by the banister. He sees a gaudy shoe and shoves. The woman falls with a cut-off scream, rolling lifelessly to the floor below.
Swearing, the second thief grabs her brother and they make their escape, collecting whatever of value they can on the way out.
Interactions
This character is a
backer-created NPC
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Edrang Hadret
CATEGORIES: Pillars of Eternity characters
URL: https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/Edrang_Hadret
======================================================================

Edrang Hadret
Biography and appearance
Game
Pillars of Eternity
Race
Meadow human
Gender
Male
Culture
Aedyr
Edrang Hadret
was a noble born Human. He was Erl of Baelreach earldom, later
gréf
of the
Dyrwood
, founder of
New Dunryd
and father of
Admeth Hadret
. Though he is best known for his part during the
Broken Stone War
.
Biography
Edrang was born in Aedyr of noble parents and spent a good part of his childhood there. His family moved to the Dyrwood as colonists while he was still a teen. He grew into a man of the people, who was well-respected by all who met him. He rose to the position of erl of
Baelreach
, which prospered under his rule. Later he was appointed gréf and tasked by the fercönyng to establish a new city to serve as center of power in the Pearlwood Gulf, the city's name was New Dunryd.
Not long after he assumed power the
Broken Stone War
erupted in the
Eir Glanfath
, and first brought him in conflict with the
Glanfathan
Galven Regd
. Erl Edrang was raised to gréf over the Dyrwood and tasked to deal with Glanfathan threat. Using unorthodox tactics he developed, he was able to countered the initial Glanfathan onslaught, though the fighting continued for five years. At the end neither Edrang nor Regd were able to gain the upper, which led to the first treaty between Glanfathans and Aedyrans being signed in
2631 AI
.
For the next 21 years, dyrwood enjoyed peace and prosperity under his rule.
In 2654, Edrang, whose health had been waning, succumbed and passed. His son Admeth assumed his father's place as gréf over the Dyrwood.
Ingame Books
Edrang Hadret (book)

NPC_BACKER_Elantien_Aldaren
Guid
469c9126-5e00-4874-b95c-938327564cfe
Elantien Aldaren
is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found in the
Hearthsong Market
.
Background
As you near, you feel a vibrant history contained in the essence of this man's soul. Voices from its past seem to call out to you.
You see a band of elves tread warily through the undergrowth, hands tight on weapons, eyes locked on the forest floor. They move silently, fluidly, a team practiced in the art of the hunt. A distant thud sends birds panicking into the sky, and the group changes course, towards the source of the sound. In the lead, a tightly-strung bowman scouts, so alert he trembles. Vaulting over an exposed root, he holds a hand in the air and the group pauses, taking cover behind time-gnarled trunks.
There's a moment of stillness before the forest itself rises up to greet them. The creature is enormous - twice the size of any elf - and its endless-seeming arms of vine snap towards its assailants, snatching at ankles, arms, legs. One elf goes down and is thrown into one of the trees with a rending crack. Another slips, jumping to avoid the snake-like vines, and before he can react a vine is around his waist. The air is blurred with arrows as the lead bowman lets loose, giving his companions time to recover. An unearthly howl sounds as a rapier slices through one of the creature's arms, incapacitating it momentarily. Taking his chance, the bowman switches arrows, lighting the wet shafts with slick blue fire. The monstrous mass of vines screeches as the flames take their toll, lashing out at any and all who come near. Two elves lie unconscious on the ground, another dead, an enormous dent in his skull.
The creature falls, finally, but there are no cheers, no celebration. The group is somber as they collect their dead and wounded.
Interactions
This character is a
backer-created NPC
.

is a noblewoman in
Pillars of Eternity
. She can be found north-east of the fountain in
Brackenbury
during
The Final Act
.
Background
This dwarven woman is dressed and perfumed like a member of the nobility, but her sun-reddened skin and sweat-stained outfit suggest that she's been out here for quite some time.
She was recently involved with
Kendal
, a young human who took a liking to her. Their relationship was primarily about having fun, sex and otherwise, and lasted for a few months before Kendal found employment with
Lumdala
's troupe, disappearing without a trace and breaking Elcga's heart (although the dwarfess would not admit to it).

NPC_BACKER_Elfwyn_Winterwolf

39774afe-87cd-446a-9cab-571ec77e68c5
Elfwyn Winterwolf
is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found in
Hearthsong
, near the main gate.
Background
As you near, you feel a vibrant history contained in the essence of this man's soul. Voices from its past seem to call out to you.
You hear it before you see it. A scream rends its way through the air as what was once a woman falls to the ground, a mess of blood and bone. Another falls to the right, paralyzed by a force far beyond her control as she is halved by a magical blade. Above, two corpses are caught in a web, cocooned and fed upon by a bevy of giant spiders, glutting themselves on the kill. Beneath them a man stands frozen in panic and grief, unaware of the spider descending silently down on a thick rope of webbing. He is broadsided by another man just in time, who knocks him out of the way but is unable to avoid the spider himself. The spider snatches him up and sinks two fangs the size of bull horns into him, eliciting a terrible shriek.
Another man laughs, weaving his spells into a night of death and betrayal.
The last man, the one who was saved, scrambles to his feet and runs, throat coarse from screaming, eyes shot with panic and tears. He looks over his shoulder, his eyes lingering on a woman's torso, unrecognizable in death, a ring on its finger matching one on his. Then he turns back toward the exit and does not look back again.
Interactions
This character is a
backer-created NPC
.

Character ID

80667cd9-37fe-4b41-9f0f-aa94f5f22aa4
Elvara Zylthain
is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
, and a member of
The Forgotten
. She can be found in the center of
Aedelwan Bridge
during
The Forgotten
.
Background
An elven mage, with a particularly ruthless streak.
As you near, you feel a vibrant history contained in the essence of this woman's soul. Voices from its past seem to call out to you.
You see a crowd, walking down a narrow road through a town. They are jeering and booing a woman who walks before them, straight-backed and stern. Some people in the crowd throw fruit and small rocks at her, others shout epithets. Her arms hang by her sides, unmoving. By all accounts, her appearance is disconnected as her face shows no sign of emotion. Her hands, clenched so hard her knuckles have turned white, betray her.
Her tormentors follow her across a small marketplace, toward the edge of town. Her face never loses its blank expression, even as the jeers become more boisterous and hateful. They reach the town limits and a man steps forward from the crowd, a large parchment in his hand. He silences the crowd and calls for the woman to face him. She does, slowly turning, making a point to stare down anyone who dares meet her gaze.
The man unrolls the parchment and reads loud enough for all to hear. He details her transgressions, speaks to her of the problems she has caused the village, and chastises her for the people she injured. He then asks her if she has any final words before she is banished forever. She stares at him, face set, unblinking. Then, without saying a word, she turns and walks away, leaving the village behind.
Interactions
This character is a
backer-created NPC
.
This character is
involved in
quests
.
The Forgotten
Quests
The Forgotten
: Target of the quest.

Eorn
is a male
orlan
in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found in the southwestern section of the

under

in

Background
This small orlan man appears to have pulled together some crates and scraps of cloth to make a rather sorry-looking little camp. The thick fur on his limbs is matted, and his clothing smeared with blackening grime. He looks startled as you enter, his eyes widening in alarm.
Eorn has been hiding in the catacombs beneath
Copperlane
for some time after he accidentally (by his own claim) struck a Crucible Knight, killing him. The whole debacle occurred during a heated discussion about shipping lanes and the man supposedly fell in a way that made it impossible for him to survive. Regardless of the reason behind his choice, Eorn took to hiding  in the catacombs, hoping to put together enough money to flee the city and avoid the Knights' brand of justice.

ec18a05b-3e03-4b94-aa9b-c97e9a974e3c
Erona
is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
. She can be found on the small island to the east of
Elmshore
, during the quest
A Servant of Death
.
Background
An elf stands on a dais in the middle of a clearing. The ground is blanketed with leaves in various shades of russet and gold. Her eyes are closed and her arms folded in front of her. A curtain of autumnal yellow hair hangs in front of her face. As you draw near, she lifts her chin and looks at you with shifting hazel eyes.
Erona is ancient even by elven standards, using the magic of the Ovates to strengthen her body and soul, extending her lifespan practically indefinitely. Her unique perspective on life and history shapes her personality and way of speaking, resulting in answers that are cryptic at best and incomprehensible at worst.
The Ovates are split on reverence for the mistress of their order, with some considering her a visionary prophet and others an old fool who's centuries past her prime, yet continues to strangle the order by insisting on remaining in a central, leadership role.
Berath
is in the latter camp and the High Ovate, while believing that she acts as a pillar of the Ovates responsible for upholding their traditions and helping them grow, is not averse to logic or persuasion.
Interactions
This character is
involved in
quests
.
A Servant of Death
Quests
A Servant of Death
: Berath asks you to return her to the Wheel. The Watcher can point out that she grows at the expense of the other Ovates, stunting their growth.

Mercenary Captain Esmar
is a treasure hunter in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found near the center of the east platform in
Northweald
during
At the Mercy of the Tribes
.
Biography
The man in blood-soaked armor points his blade at you as his wounded and weary comrades flock into position behind him.
Esmar is a
Dyrwood
native, and has a healthy few months-old son at home (oddly enough, not
hollowborn
). However, his potential career as a father is in danger, as he leads an illegal treasure-hunting expedition in
Glanfathan
territory, looting
Engwithan
ruins which are sacred to the Glanfathans. He says he does it so his son can have a good life. He also hopes that his son will be smarter than he is, and avoid trying to get rich by looting Eir Glanfath.
At the same time, he is a man of honor - a rare trait in a mercenary. If he can save his soldiers by surrendering himself and being taken prisoner, then he will do so without a second thought.

Character ID
NPC_Ethelmoer

3612e842-1aed-4792-93b1-ca4a37712df8
Head Warden Ethelmoer
is an
animancer
and administrator in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found on the first floor of the
Sanitarium
in the
Brackenbury
district of
Defiance Bay
(in the form of a statue).
Background
A well-maintained statue of a middle-aged man presides over the room, looking down unblinking over the bustle of harried academics pestered by the hopeful desperate.
A strong warmth emanates from it that you could sense from across the room. There is a human soul stored beneath the stonework. You feel its attention turn towards you.
The statue is Ethelmoer, a scientist who dedicated his life to the pursuit of knowledge in the discipline of
animancy
, and became the head warden of the Brackenbury Sanitarium.
Ethelmoer was one of the most talented animancers a century ago, responsible for running the sanitarium since its inception. When age began to creep up on him, he realized that death would be an unfitting end for a life spent in pursuit of discovery. For the soul to move on, all of its experience wiped out, it was improper and selfish. As such, he endeavored to transfer his soul to a statue carved in his likeness. The simple procedure allowed his soul to remain in Eora, amassing knowledge and running the sanitarium for 94 year straight.
His one point of contention is the fact that he can't take an active role in scholarly efforts, as while sometimes animancers humor him and ask for a consult, most are jealous and afraid of others stealing credit for their work. As such, Ethelmoer focuses on administrative duties.
Interactions
This character is
involved in
quests
.
The Man Who Waits
This character has
other interactions
.
He can give you a history lesson about the sanitarium.
Quests
The Man Who Waits
: During the main quest
The Man Who Waits
, you need to ask him for permission to enter the
Patient Ward
.

Character ID
NPC_Lady_Webb
Guid
939cfa7e-0b7e-4678-8646-c0b1bbc2e037
Lady Eydis Webb
is the founder and leader of the
Dunryd Row
spy agency in
Pillars of Eternity
. She can be found on the second floor of
Hadret House
in the
Brackenbury
district of
Defiance Bay
.
Background
This woman clutches a thick stack of parchment with spindly, wrinkled fingers, their flesh stretched thin to near translucence with extreme age, a gossamer veil over pale blue veins. Ink splotches and candle burns dot and stripe her hands like a quilt patterned after some great, indescribable truth.
She does not look up from her reading.
Lady Eydis Webb is the head of
Dunryd Row
, an elderly woman surrounded by mystery and lies, the spider at the center of the web. She gathers knowledge through any and all available means: Exhaustive research, spying, bribery, and less savory measures. Whatever the knowledge demands, she pays it in full, for nothing has greater value. Beyond her skill as tactician and analyst, she is also an immensely powerful
cipher
who prolonged her life through a variety of procedures to ensure she lived long enough to finish her job and learn as much as possible. The accumulated experience and power bore fruit when she "convinced" the reigning duc to fund the
Dunryd Row
in 2800 AI, installing her at the head of a secret network of gifted investigators. This position allowed her to work towards protecting the Dyrwood and its independence with the full power of the duc and his erls at her back.
Her most recent focus is the
Leaden Key
, a terrorist organization targeting animancy and Defiance Bay. Her attempts to get an agent inside their ranks failed, losing four ciphers in 2823 AI alone.
It is more than just a professional matter for her. Lady Webb is a former member of the organization and one-time lover of
Thaos ix Arkannon
. Before Dunryd Row, the lady had many suitors, all fine-looking men of good stock. Her raw power as a cipher allowed her to peer into their thoughts and realize that they were shallow, unimpressive. She toyed with them while resenting each and every one. It changed when she met Thaos,   a man whose mind was a mystery to her. He understood her, knew her at a glance, told her things she didn't know about herself. Although neither handsome nor charming, he was all she  ever wanted. He used her gift, asking her about seemingly random people and learning their secrets.
It was only a matter of time until she was intiated into the Key. When one day he asked her if she trusted him, she said she did. Then she was donning a silver mask and swearing oaths to
Woedica
, using her gift to advance their agenda. However,  her curious mind chafed under the secrecy demanded by the Key. Living in the dark is a powerless, hopeless feeling. Every day she did things without knowing their purpose. Every day she loved a man without knowing his thoughts of her, sensing that there were things he wanted to tell her. She sensed that he knew everything the Leaden Key was created to hide. But Thaos was unyielding.
So one day she disappeared without a word. He would kill her for it and she knew that. She just wanted to see him plunge the knife, because then she would have her answers. But the day never came. He has left her to wonder. It inspired her to devote her years to learning everything she could about him, in hopes she would find some clue as to who he really was, and why he felt he had to keep his secrets locked away. While she failed at understanding Thaos, she succeeded in a tangential effort, the creation of the greatest intelligence agency in the world.
Interactions
This character is
involved in
quests
.
The Hermit of Hadret House
Quests
The Hermit of Hadret House
: Lady Webb enlists your aid in learning more of the Leaden Key's activities, guiding the Watcher's efforts. As the animancy hearings near, however, she unwittingly attracts the attention of Thaos. When the grandmaster assassinates Duc Aevar and the Row is occupied trying to contain the damage, lady Webb is left alone at Hadret House. Thaos visits her. They share a moment and then he kills her, a death long in coming. In that instant, however, he also opens his mind to her, granting Lady Webb the answer she sought for so long. Although the Watcher can witness their exchange through the medium of Webb's soul, they do not learn what the answer was.
Gallery

======================================================================
PAGE: Falanroed
CATEGORIES: Pillars of Eternity characters
URL: https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/Falanroed
======================================================================

Falanroed

213b7493-de96-48e7-982e-b491f895283a
9f6817c1-6e1e-49ff-a809-0feb3e331a73
Falanroed
is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
. She can be found at the Shrine of Galawain in the northeastern corner of
Black Meadow
, after you agree to help
Sefyra
during
The Master Below
.
Background
A woman in gleaming mail stands guard before the shrine, her head bowed in thought or prayer. The clasp of her cloak bears the engraving of a dog's head.
Falanroed is the last member of the Scorched Blade, a rag-tag team of dragon slayers devoted to
Galawain
. They worked hard to ensure they remained drake slayers at best, though they did once have to fight a dragon. They won, obviously, but the group unraveled shortly thereafter, with most of its members joining either the Hounds of Galawain or the Society of the Talon. Falanroed didn't care for looting old temples or preening nobles, so she stayed in her old occupation... Though she recently feels more like a priest of Galawain than any notable dragon slayer.
Interactions
This character is
involved in
quests
.
The Master Below
Quests
The Master Below
: The mighty, strong-spirited Falanroed is the perfect candidate for the
adra dragon
to take over. But she won't give up easily.

Farentis
is the previous High Abbot of
the Abbey of the Fallen Moon
in
The White March - Part II
. He's now of the Low Tide, awaiting the next Rising inside the
Halls of Silence
.
Background
Tufts of downy gray hair sprout from the back of this man's scalp like shrubs. His lower lip curls inward over a row of teeth that appear to have mostly fallen out, and a musty odor hangs about him like a fog.
Farentis was the High Abbot of the Abbey of the Fallen Moon preceeding
Kaoto
. Having been of the High Tide in his time as Abbot, he is now of the Low Tide and dwells in the Abbey's lower levels, in the
Halls of Silence
. The effects of the Halls have driven him to dementia, though unlike his peers, he is of sound mind and desires only one thing: To die in peace outside the Halls of Silence.
Interactions
This character is
involved in
quests
.
The Rising Tide
Quests
The Rising Tide
:  He asks for an alternative: Be freed of the Halls of Silence by bringing him
Ondra's Witness
. He also gives you the
cracked ornate key
to open doors without annoying his fellow Low Tide members.

Feara
is the only survivor of a failed relic hunter expedition to
Durgan's Battery
in
The White March - Part I
. She can be found in
The Gréf's Rest
in
Stalwart Village
.
Background
Feara raises a frosty mug to you. Even though the warmth of the hearth reaches you here, her hands are wrapped in wyr wool and her ears muffled by a hat. Her face has the taut, wind-beaten look of someone who's recently come from the road.
Although she claims she was a part of a relic-retrieval expedition, that's only half-true: She is actually a
svef
smuggler who lost her
shipment
in the lagufaeth attack and needs someone to retrieve it... Before her supplier decides that she ripped him off.

Character ID
NPC_Ferwlt
Guid
26a4f719-bf8a-4664-bb13-8a7b288580cd
Ferwlt
is a
Glanfathan
chieftain in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be encountered during a vision while talking to
Bethwl
in
The Passage of the Six
in the
Hearthsong
district of
Twin Elms
.
Background
Suddenly, you feel the presence of someone else in the hall. The anamenfath continues to glare at you, but something has stepped out of her skin. It reaches out to you.
A vision of an orlan man appears near Bethwl. He has the same green-brown fur and hazel eyes as she does. "Help me reason with them." He points behind you.
Turning, you see the ghostly shapes of five other anamfatha - another orlan with tawny fur and a scarred face, a frowning dwarf, a black-furred orlan, and two elves.
The anamenfath of the Broken Compass tribe at the time of the
Broken Stone War
, Ferwlt believed that the invaders could be taught to respect the Builders as we do. He also believed that the Builders' souls had spread to all peoples and that we should avoid needless conflict with others. More practically, he worried that a violent response would only spur further bloodshed across the generations.
Interactions
This character is
involved in
quests
.
The Assassin at Large
Quests
The Assassin at Large
: The ghostly apparition of Ferwlt appears to aid the Watcher in convincing the anamenfath to give access to Twin Elms.

Wael

Firedorn
is the leader of a group of dragon cultists in
The White March - Part I
. He can be found by the north-east campsite in
Longwatch Falls
during
Bounty: Disciples of the True Flame
.
Background
He leads a group called the "Disciples of the True Flame", which worships
dragons
and tries to kill anyone who hunts dragons/drakes/wurms.
Interactions
This character is
involved in
quests
.
Bounty: Disciples of the True Flame
Quests
Bounty: Disciples of the True Flame
: You can kill him to collect the bounty on his head.
Behind the scenes
Firedorn is consistently referred to in the game using male pronouns, but has a female character model.
His addition may have been an in-joke referring to the
Firedorn Lightbringer
controversy which took place a few months before the release of the expansion, but this hasn't been confirmed by the developers.

is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found in the northwestern corner of
Magran's Fork
.
Background

Gilded Vale
who thought he could find luck in
Defiance Bay
and
Madhmr Bridge
, Foelmar just made camp in Magran's Fork to rest and relax.

This character has
other interactions
.
Talking to Foelmar triggers an attack from a wolf. After killing it, he gives you 3x
Potion of Minor Endurance
and reconsiders his trip.

Foreman Ismey
is a miner in
The White March - Part II
.
She can be found near the entrance inside
Stalwart Mines
, when you first enter them. Later, she can be found in the
Foreman's Quarters
to the left of the entrance inside
Stalwart Mines
.
Background
A stern-faced woman approaches you. Her already impressive frown is only emphasized by her slight underbite. She folds her arms.
Foreman Ismey oversees the other miners of the
Stalwart Mines
, taking over from the previous overseers. As the only game in town, the mine is going to be the future basis of Stalwart's and the Battery's economy. No pressure.
Interactions
This character
starts
quests
.
Whispers in the Dark
Quests
Whispers in the Dark
: When you first enter the mines, she approaches you and tells you about a recent murder and some missing miners, which starts the side-quest.

NPC_Freyol

Freyol
is a psychiatric patient in
Pillars of Eternity
. She can be found in the
Patient Ward
of the
Sanitarium
in the
Brackenbury
district of
Defiance Bay
(in the main room at the center of the map).
Background
A curved spine twists this woman's posture forward, her greasy hair draped over her large eyes like vines. She smiles at you.
Freyol is a commoner of low material status, with a rather sad past: Her husband and both sons died in the
Saint's War
, and her daughter was
hollowborn
. She managed to survive by renting a small place in
Ondra's Gift
, mending clothes and taking alms from others. However, her life was complicated further by a seemingly simple job: A noblewoman brought her a dazzling blue gown to repair... The color caused her soul to awake, bringing back forth memories of a noble life, filled with food, mansions, and men doting over her. She could not reconcile those memories of privilege and carelessness with the harsh circumstances of her current life. It threatened to drive her crazy, so she checked herself into the
Sanitarium
for treatment, to try and forget.
Interactions
This character has
other interactions
.
Offers feedback on other patients and background on
Caedman Azo

======================================================================
PAGE: Fulvano
CATEGORIES: Pillars of Eternity characters, Pillars of Eternity mentioned-only characters
URL: https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/Fulvano
======================================================================

Fulvano
Biography and appearance
Game
Pillars of Eternity
Race
Ocean human
Gender
Male
Culture
Vailian
Gameplay
Location
Anslög's Compass
Drops
Fulvano's Letter
Fine Arbalest
Fulvano
is a famous explorer hailing from the
Vailian Republics
.
Background
He is known as an eccentric traveller with a wish to see the world, and he prefers to travel by foot wherever possible, claiming to do so in order to gain the locals' appreciation of the sights, sounds, and smells of the lands he passed through. In practice, however, Fulvano often claimed that his explorations proved only that the world outside of the Vailian Republics was a foul, smelly place that paled in comparison to his homeland.
His last voyage took him to the
Eastern Reach
and the Grasp. Traveling around
Gilded Vale
Fulvano would give each of these travellers a gift, namely some of his own travelling gear, before parting with them, in order to help them on their own journeys. All three of these travellers can be found killed by wildlife in various locations not far from where Fulvano describes meeting them. His final goal was
Anslög's Compass
, where poor Fulvano experienced the height of misfortune. Apart from disappointment in the state of the lagoon (which he found underwhelming), he lost the bulk of his letters when he tripped over some driftwood, then when he tried to inspect the local dragon skulls arranged by xaurips, the tribe drove him off.
Ultimately, the tribe would end up driving a spear into his back as he sat writing an anguished
letter chronicling his misfortune
.
Interactions
This character has no special interactions.
Other interactions
Fulvano's "set" can be collected, consisting of:
Fulvano's Amulet
(
Magran's Fork
)
Fulvano's Boots
(
Black Meadow
)
Fulvano's Gloves
(
Valewood
)
Fulvano's Blunderbuss
(
Anslög's Compass
)
A number of his pages can be found, documenting the whereabouts of each of the items and the people they were given to (with the exception of his blunderbuss, which was added in a later patch):
Page from Fulvano's Travels (amulet clue)
Page from Fulvano's Travels (boots clue)
Page from Fulvano's Travels (gloves clue)
Fulvano's Letter
, along with a
Chewed Letter
and a
Scrawled Letter
, both accounts of the fate of Fulvano's travelling companions.
In Avowed, adventuring gear used by Fulvano to explore
The Living Lands
can be found in
Sanza's Emporium
.
Behind the scenes
During the
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
crowdfunding campaign, a map of "
Fulvano's Voyage
" in the Deadfire Archipelago was revealed. Each location Fulvano discovered on the map was unlocked at certain stretch goal tiers, to be included as actual locations in the game upon release. At the end of this event, the final map image shows a note written by Fulvano stating that he planned to retire to the Eastern Reach (where the first game takes place). This seems to also imply that he created the map before the events of the first game, then traveled to the Eastern Reaches where he then died.

Fyrga
is a tranquil Ardent of
Magran
in
Pillars of Eternity
. She can be found in the
Ducal Palace
in the
First Fires
district of
Defiance Bay
, at the
Shrine of Magran
.
Background
Fyrga is the Tranquil Ardent, or head priest of the shrine to Magran at the ducal palace. Though ostensibly a fervent believer in the gods, she is suffering a crisis of faith. As a result, the sacred pyre of Magran's statue isn't lit, as well as for all other statues through all districts of
Defiance Bay
. Fyrga simply isn't able to keep those fires lit. After fifteen years of faithful service to Magran, the Hollowborn Crisis has dearly affected her: How could Magran allow her favored nation to suffer like this? What strength can a soulless child offer?
However, she believes that Magran may be speaking to her again: After having visions of a
cave
, she hopes
someone
will help her, to go looking after some token of her
deity
in that dangerous place, that might
restore her faith
. Until then, she serves the people as best she can... That is, not at all, as her faith has crumbled.
Interactions
This character
starts
quests
.
Cinders of Faith
Quests
Cinders of Faith
: When the Watcher returns with the
ember of faith
from her visions, their own faith and way of looking at things may decide, whether Fyrga gains her faith back or abdicates her role.
Companion reactions
Durance
is rather nonplussed about Fyrga's lack of faith:
“
What strength do we gain from adversity? From trial and test?
"The fires don't wither because she lacks in faith, they wither because she does not understand how to be faithful to a whore.
"A whore wants neither praise nor devotion. A whore wants to be compensated for her services, and Magran is paid and pleasured in sweat and struggle and pain. This... mewling imbecile is unworthy of Magran's womanly heat.
[...]
"Hnh. Worthless, like so many of Magran's flock. Afraid of her god's tests. If Magran speaks to her, it is to hasten an end to a hypocrite's life, and this vision is surely a deathtrap for the weak."
”
Trivia
Despite being a priest of
Magran
, Fyrga is a
fighter
-class character.

Fîorm
is a dead hunter in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found at the base of the waterfall in the center of
Northweald
.
Background
Beneath the raging waterfall, the corpse of an elven Glanfathan hunter lies shattered against the rocks. Broken bones protrude through its skin, but you also notice deep, parallel cuts in the rotting flesh.
The slashes trace almost every part of the body, as if it had been ravaged by a dozen blades. This man probably died before his fall from the cliffs above.
Fiorm took part in the traditional blood hunt of his people, together with
Arthwn
of the
Twice-Split Arrows
. He regarded his partner with disdain and deliberately lured him away from the great stelgaer they were hunting, desiring to gain the glory for himself. Unfortunately, he overestimated his skill - although he killed the stelgaer, he did so at the cost of his own life. Artwn returned and claimed the glory of killing the stelgaer, while lying that Fiorm fled into exile - a strange turn of events where the cheater became the cheated.
Ridai, the brother of Fiorm, took offence to that and demanded an inquiry.
Interactions
This character is
involved in
quests
.
Hunter, Brother
Quests
Hunter, Brother
: He is found at the bottom of the waterfall... It turns out, he did really kill the Stelgaer.

Character ID
NPC_BACKER_Gaius_Firemane
Guid
16a39297-02c6-40c3-a850-dcefd5a930fa
Gaius Firemane
is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found in the western room of
The Celestial Sapling
, in the
Hearthsong
district of
Twin Elms
.
Background
As you near, you feel a vibrant history contained in the essence of this man's soul. Voices from its past seem to call out to you.
You see a young redheaded boy - almost but not quite a man - stalking the steps of a panther, an ugly curved dagger in his hand. The panther treads lightly, jumping from rock to rock, unconcerned by the danger behind him. He bounds up a tree and turns his green-slit eyes on the child, and you could swear he would be laughing if he could. A flash of black fur and the panther is gone and the boy's thoughts littered with curses and self-doubt.
The boy moves on, walking for days, hunting for something he never seems to find. He sleeps only in fits, a few hours at a time before he continues, exhausted and starving. Another day passes and he kills a rabbit, it's huge brown eyes almost entranced as the boy slits its throat from ear to ear and begins to eat it raw. He winces as the tough meat goes down, coagulated blood running down his chin.
Covered in drying blood and fatigue, he falls asleep beneath a nearby outcropping of rock, determined to continue the hunt when he awakes. He does not have to wait long - a snuffling in the undergrowth nearby and his eyes are instantly open, a small hand on his blade. The great black bear sizes him up, almost triple his size, and goes back to foraging. The boy grasps his dagger, knuckles white and breath in short, nervous gasps.
It takes almost a full day before it is done and he collapses, arm dangling limply from his side. He calls hoarsely, voice feeble. Three men come out from behind the outcropping, swollen with pride. No longer a boy, the young man begins to skin the bear with their help.
Interactions
This character is a
backer-created NPC
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Galven Regd
CATEGORIES: Pillars of Eternity characters
URL: https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/Galven_Regd
======================================================================

Galven Regd
Biography and appearance
Game
Pillars of Eternity
Race
Orlan
Gender
Male
Culture
Glanfathan
Galven Regd
was a
Glanfathan
orlan, fabled freedom fighter or a dirty-fighting orlan depending on which side of the
Bael River
you ask. He fought in the
Broken Stone War
as a member of the
Fangs
, after which he become
galven
of the
Eir Glanfath
and remained so until the
War of Black Trees
.
Biography
Nothing is know of Regd before the Broken Stone War after which he distinguished himself with his ferocity and swift action. The actual war was short, though after he was appointed galven Regd continued the attacks on the colonists. The erls of
Dyrwood
unable to stop his attacks elected
Edrang Hadret
as
gréf
, and so started their famous rivalry. During the course of two years they fought each other to a stalemate which forced the Dyrwoodans to sign a treaty with the promise to stop the plundering of
Engwithan
ruins.
The peace lasted until
2631 AI
when Dyrwoodan erls broke the treaties and sent men to plunder the ruins. Galven Regd again railed the Glanfathans, and even managed to convice a number of
delemgan
to join the fight. This time the Dyrwoodans were lead by
Admeth Hadret
, the son of his former adversary. At one time during the war Regd was in the forest at the
Isce Uar River
when Admeth proved a shrewd enemy. He set the forest on fire and blocked the retreat, which led to the death of thousands Glanfathans and Regd's capture.
After his capture Galven Regd was brought to
New Heomar
, and the Glanfathans lost the war. His ultimate fate is unknown.
Ingame Books
Galven Regd (book)

21d14f96-5da0-4711-acae-0b3d89a541b6
Galvino
is an engineer and
animancer
in
The White March - Part I
. He can be found in the basement of
Galvino's Cabin
, in the
Durgan's Battery
exterior map.
Background
An elderly man hunches over a cluttered desk in the back of the workshop. His flesh is pulled tightly around thin, fragile-looking bones, except around his neck, where it hangs in loose wattles. He turns as you enter, a scowl already chiseled into his lined face.
Galvino is a Vailian animancer who sought the privacy of the White March for his work.  His first place of work was
Stalwart village
, where he crossed paths with  the
Devil of Caroc
, condemned to die for her vengeful streak and path of destruction she carved across the March. Galvino was convinced that it would be wasteful to kill her outright, so he convinced Mayor
Cyneheod
to allow him to perform an experiment. Galvino transferred a fully intact soul from a living subject to a fabricated body with no loss of personality or memory. The body was his life's greatest, most sophisticated work, the transfer the crowning of his career as an animancer. But the villagers disagreed after
Grynde
revealed the deal and ran him, the Mayor, and the
Devil of Caroc
out of Stalwart.
Unwilling to roll over and give up, Galvino set up in an abandoned underground near the Battery, where he commenced work on replicating his success with the Devil, attempting to create more golems like her. However, despite intense work and importing of specimens from the Brackenbury
sanitarium
, he was stuck at a dead-end: The constructs were misshapen, broken, or explosive, with no sign of the intelligence that he hoped to implement in them. Without a success to demonstrate before the academies of science in the
Vailian Republics
, he is nobody (and that cushy job at Solano won't be available).
However, years of study left him the foremost expert on the Battery and the Pargrunen dwarves that built it. Meticulous research and gathering of facts allowed him to discern the precise methods for opening the Battery's doors, although due to a distinct lack of living Battery dwarves, he has not been able to test his theory (the doors require a cantec, a statement of purpose, to open up). He assumes that all that's needed is finding and awakening the soul that belonged to one such dwarf and then recording the cantec. Just a simple, everyday job.
Interactions
This character is
involved in
quests
.
The Recluse of the White March
The Fisherman's Penance
The One that Got Away
Quests
The Recluse of the White March
: Galvino is the only one who knows how to access the Battery.
The One that Got Away
The Fisherman's Penance
: He wants you to take revenge on the man who wronged him: Grynde.
Gallery

Gamel
is a miner in
The White March - Part II
. He can be found in the
Stalwart Mines
, restrained on the floor of the
Foreman's Quarters
to the left of the entrance.
Background
A fresh immigrant to Stalwart, Gamel works as a drags-man in the
Stalwart Mines
- i.e. he loads, transports, and unload carts of ore. He only moved to Stalwart recently, when the mines reopened.
Just now he was found hunched over the dead body of another miner, Saemic, with the victim's blood all over him and pieces of the victim's flesh on his fingernails and teeth – but he doesn't remember anything of the encounter, or the few hours before that.
Foreman Ismey
has apprehended him on charge of murder.

“
My forefathers taught the citizens of Defiance Bay to fear and respect the Doemenel name. In the wake of Hadret's Rebellion, establishing a reputation was... most expedient. Lord Gedmar Doemenel
is a nobleman and crime boss in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found on the second floor of
Doemenel Manor
in the
Brackenbury
district of
Defiance Bay
.
Background
Lord Gedmar Doemenel is a man of presence. He wears finery in the style of Aedyre nobility, yet there are scars across his knuckles and burns on his neck that few highborn bear.
Gedmar is the head of the Doemenel family, and boss of the family's criminal organization. He is a shrewd strategist, anticipating major upheavals in the power structure governing the Bay and wants his family to retake their place under the sun... While avoiding the devastation that's sure to follow when the
Crucible Knights
and the
Dozens
finally come to blows.

Giacco
is a prisoner at

.
Background
A young man who has suffered unjustly in the dungeons of Lord Raedric VII, Giacco was a member of Kolsc's team that tried infiltrating the stronghold through the dungeons and failed. Captured and imprisoned, he was forced to watch as his friends were used by
Osrya
to test various approaches to developing a cure for Waidwen's Legacy. All of them failed and the poor Giacco was sure he was the next to fall.

Character ID

Guid
e51ee75a-8f88-4fee-94cf-ce733c3c0695
Glasvahl
is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found near
Rymrgand's Gate
in the main chamber of
Noonfrost
in the
Oldsong
district of
Twin Elms
.
Background
As you near the platform, the temperature drops sharply. Something saps every last bit of warmth from the air, leaving the breath of your lungs cold as it escapes your lips.
You see a doorway filled in with glowing ice. Silhouetted against it is an elven man, his arms outstretched.
Glasvahl leads a large clan of
Glamfellen
who migrated to Noonfrost seeking entry into the White Void and permanent death, after a random visit from a Watcher revealed that they were all reborn intact over the twelve thousand years they inhabited the White, no closer to Rymrgand's mercy. The shock of this revelation prompted them to awaken on the following night, driving them to the extremes of endurance.
Interactions
Pillars of Eternity
This character is
involved in
quests
.
Into the White Void
Quests
Into the White Void
: You are asked by Rymrgand to dissuade the elves from their plans.
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
If he is allowed entry into the White Void, he can be encountered again while in the
Realm of the Beast of Winter
during
A Glimpse Beyond
. Speaking to him will allow the Watcher to conclude that they are in Rymrgand's realm. He is satisfied and is awaiting his final end. He will seemingly crumble to dust after the conversation.
Speaking to
Rymrgand
afterwards will reveal that he still remains "here, lingering in the In-Between, refusing rebirth and dissolution both. A most irritating spirit."
Gallery
Glasvahl in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire

Sagani
Gordy
is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found near the south gate of the
Copperlane
district of
Defiance Bay
.
Background
A young boy watches the passersby and counts a grimy handful of coins. His face and arms are smudged with dirt, but except for the grass stains, his clothes are in good condition.
As you approach, he blinks and makes a quick, furtive effort to pocket his coins.
He is
Hamor
's son and a kid who's incredibly passionate about knives. He learned a lot about metalworking, about how they are made in a forge, how they can pierce armor... His biggest ambition is to have a
March Steel Dagger
, one of the finest blades to be had in Defiance Bay (except for Durgan steel, but they don't make it anymore, so it doesn't count).

Gram
is a psychiatric patient in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found in the
North Ward
of the
Sanitarium
in the
Brackenbury
district of
Defiance Bay
(in the third cell on the left).
Background
This man has the look in his eyes of a hunted animal, and at your approach, he staggers backward to the far corner of his cell, halfway appearing as though he would climb the walls if it would get him farther away from you.
Gram is a man in his early thirties, rendered mute by the  over-fragmentation of his soul. His inability to communicate through words made him the perfect choice for
Caedman Azo
's research, causing him severe pain and induced panic, resulting in Azo having guards bring him in.
Restrained and attached to animantic machines, Azo ruined his soul further, but not before he witnessed
Thaos
' body-surfing from a
wicht
into a
flesh construct
, all to converse with
Uscgrim
.
Interactions
This character is
involved in
quests
.
The Man Who Waits
Quests
The Man Who Waits
: In order to get to him you have to advance the quest
The Man Who Waits
to gain access to the
North Ward
. You can explore his horrifying experiences using Watcher powers... And learn just how badly mangled his soul is. You'll end up having to kill him, because
Thaos
opens his cell and uses mind-control to make him attack you.

Character ID
NPC_Gramrfel

fb035336-7c5d-4119-b958-ec19d53a3529
“
If the soul is what determines life, then death is not the blade through the heart or the poison in the drink. It is the fragmentation of a soul. The destruction of a soul - indeed, the absence of it. These... Hollowborn are the closest thing to true Death we shall ever come upon. ...I only wonder if it is the culmination of Berath's work, or an escape from it.
”
Gramrfel the Wayfarer
is a bandit in
Pillars of Eternity
. He can be found near the crossroads at
Magran's Fork
after you have been to
Defiance Bay
and return.
Background
A motley gathering stands at the crossroads. At first glance the person at the head of the group seems more monster than man. Two large and curling horns emerge from the sides of his skull, forming a hardened carapace that is pocked like coral.
Down its center this strange shell splits, revealing the ash-gray skin of a man's face, obscured above the nose. The horns might seem a mere helmet, save that in several places you cannot tell where the flesh ends and the bone begins.
From the neck down, however, this is by all appearances a human man, clad in fine armor of the same dark shade as his features.
Gramrfel is a brigand of uncanny education and sophistication, yet a brigand all the same. He traveled the world and spent some time among the lore chanters. Deeply fascinated by death, he appears to travel in search of catastrophes, regarding the Hollowborn as the closest thing to true death.
Interactions
This character has
other interactions
.
He and his goons attack you after short conversation in the Fork.
Other interactions
When you defeat him, you can choose to imprison him provided that you have restored your stronghold
dungeons
.
If you are a Death Godlike, you can avoid the fight, but going too deep in the conversation will start the fight.
If
Pallegina
and
Kana Rua
are in your party, he has some additional dialogue with them.
Gallery
The full enemy line-up (Path of the Damned Difficulty)

Character ID
Companion_GM
Guid
b1a7e807-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
Grieving Mother
is a female
Cipher
. She is one of the possible
companions
in
Pillars of Eternity
. She can be found and recruited in the southeast corner of
Dyrford Village
.
Background
This middle-aged peasant woman is dressed in a brown leather cloth draped down to her knees. Her hands are working at separating stringy, colorless vegetables in a pile before her, stripping the heads off the long fibrous stems with a paring knife.
She discards the stems one by one, placing the heads of the vegetables into a small cradle-like basket in front of her. She doesn't greet you as you approach. You're not sure she even knows you're there.
"Excuse me?"
The woman doesn't respond, she keeps stripping the heads from the vegetables with a steady rhythm. She may be deaf - there's no indication she heard you.
At first glance, she seems nothing more than a middle-aged woman, unremarkable, maybe less stern than most, who seems more focused on the weaving in her lap than her surroundings... yet you suddenly notice she's not stripping the vegetables before her any longer, she's weaving... and the vegetable pots are now missing.
She still pays you no mind, her brown locks torn and snagged from lack of washing, like many of the townsfolk you've seen. There is a strange blur to her... even the motions of her hands seem to be playing with threads that lack color... in a shape that lacks interest.
It may be that she is half-minded... or deaf... but something feels wrong. As you watch, her knitting takes on an odd cadence, and you have a terrible suspicion that something lurks beneath what your eyes are showing you.
"Grieving Mother" is a seemingly middle-aged woman with brown hair.
Beyond that, little information is immediately given to the player. Additional insight into her backstory is provided through her companion quest and dialog as the story progresses.
Identity
Upon first meeting Grieving Mother, she remarks that
the Watcher
is the first to truly see her as she is. Using her skills as a powerful
cipher
, she is able to hide her identity from others. She refers to this as creating a caul for herself, a cloak that allows her to appear as an easily forgettable peasant.
None of the Watcher's other companions see though this illusion.
Her true name is never revealed. "Grieving Mother" is a title the Watcher unconsciously gives her.
Much of her own history is initially forgotten to her. Sometime prior to arriving in
Dyrford
she acted as a midwife to a small village located in
Dyrwood Forest
.
She would assist expectant and birthing mothers at "the birthing bell", a plateau of
adra
with a bell cradled in an arm of stone. The structure had once been abandoned by the
Glanfathan
and Grieving Mother took it as her own.
Even though she was not one, she became known as a Watcher due to her "tellings" of children's souls.
In truth, Grieving Mother would shape the minds of the children and their mothers through her cipher abilities. She based her tellings on the thoughts of the mother
and continued to shape their expectations and the child's mind over time.
Hollowborn Crisis
Grieving Mother's village was isolated and word of
Waidwen's Legacy
was slow to reach them. Fearing that she was at fault, or would be blamed for the hollowborn births or that the parents would kill the children,
Grieving Mother used her cipher abilities to make parents believe their children were healthy.
Eventually, Grieving Mother focused too greatly on maintaining the lies and succumbed to the power of her own illusions. One morning a mother was found dead, "her mind so focused on the care of her Hollowborn child that she'd neglected even to feed herself". This broke the illusion she held over the town and caused her to flee.
Interactions
This character is a
party member
.
This character
starts
quests
.
Dream and Memory
Companion
Grieving Mother can be recruited in
Dyrford Village
.
She is a powerful
Cipher
who can provide crowd control.
As a result of her hidden identity, she has almost no
banter
with other companions.
She will leave your party if you do any of the following:
Sacrifice
Saeda
to
Aldhelm
during
Undying Heritage
.
Sacrifice
Vela
during
Sacrificial Bloodlines
.
Fail to save her during the end of
Dream and Memory
.
Companion quest
Dream and Memory
: Grieving Mother is in a state of deep mourning from
Waidwen's Legacy
. Her quest starts when you meet her. It is entirely resolved through dialogue, some of which is only available after later acts.
Endings
Grieving Mother's ending slides are dependent on two events:
Successfully guiding her through
Dream and Memory
.
Choosing to either wipe her memory or not. She will request this after completing her quest and resting at least 2 times.

Initial points
Grieving Mother's initial
skill
,
talent
, and
ability
distribution depends on the level of the Watcher when she is first added to the party, but only if the "Auto Level Companions" option is checked in the difficulty settings.
With auto-levelling enabled, companions will be levelled following a preset point distribution. The player is still free to distribute points into whatever skills, abilities, and talents they desire when a companion levels up, and they may be completely retrained at an
inn
. Grieving Mother always starts at a minimum of level 2 (the stats above are before she joins the party).
With auto-levelling disabled, companions are not levelled, and the player may choose their own point distribution. Grieving Mother starts at level 1 with the requisite experience to level her up to the current player level or level 2, whichever is higher.

Behind the scenes
Grieving Mother was written by
Chris Avellone
.
She was voiced by actor
Tara Platt
, who also provided sounds for the female player character.
Before PoE 1.03, Grieving Mother had the following Attributes: Mig: 11 Con: 12 Dex: 16 Per: 17 Int: 12 Res: 10.
Durance
and Grieving Mother originally had intersecting backstories, but it didn't make it to the final game.

Grimda
is a high-ranking priestess of
Wael
in
Pillars of Eternity
. She can be found in
The Hall of Revealed Mysteries
in the
Copperlane
district of
Defiance Bay
.
Background
An elderly dwarf surveys the stacks. Her skin looks as tough and wrinkled as a walnut. Despite her stature, she manages to look down her nose at you.
As the High Archivist of the Hall of Revealed Mysteries, she is both a priestess and a scholar... Though her age has left her with precious little time for time-wasters and laymen. Especially in the past few weeks, when a group of thieves made off with a
scroll of Wael
, intending to blaspheme by selling that which should remain hidden.
Grimda is fundamentally opposed to
animancy
, considering it a dangerous discipline. As such, she routinely denies access to the Elder Archives when researchers such as
Nedyn
request access to texts contained within.
Interactions
This character
starts
quests
.

This character is
involved in
quests
.

Quests

: She asks you track down and bring a group of thieves to justice, before they can blaspheme against Wael.

Character ID
PX1_NPC_Grynde
Guid
d21a2a1d-d19d-4152-beff-b94e1642dca5
Grynde
is a fisherman in
The White March - Part I
. He can be found inside the
Fishery
in
Stalwart Village
.
Background
Grynde is the head fisherman of
Stalwart Village
and  has a drug addiction problem – which some of the other villagers know about, but they turn a blind eye because his work in the fishery is so important for the village's survival.
However, he has a darker secret yet: In the past he has resorted to stealing in order to pay for drugs, and when his sister found out and threatened to tell everybody, he killed her (he was high at the time and shoved her too forcefully, resulting in fatal injuries).
Interactions
This character is
involved in
quests
.
The Fisherman's Penance
Quests
The Fisherman's Penance
: You can discover his secret , whereupon he will arrive and ask you to stay quiet about it. You can let him off the hook, extort him, convince him to come clean, or kill him.

Hair of Ondra
is a "character" in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Background
The Hair of Ondra are an unique manifestation of the eponymous goddess, appearing as columns of water, writhing and shifting in the air. They usually appear gigantic in size, according to sailors who are unfortunate enough to encounter them on the high seas.

Hamond
is a merchant in
The White March - Part II
. He can be found in
Hamond's Emporium
in
Stalwart Village
once you've started
The White March - Part II
story-line.
Background
The shopkeeper's clothes seem a little nicer - if not terribly warm - compared to what you've seen around Stalwart. His hair is immaculately coiffed, and his rosy cheeks look like they've barely been touched by the mountain winds.
Hamon is a classic example of a slippery snake, selling cheap goods at inflated prices to the people of Stalwart who haven't seen anything better in a long, long while.
Interactions
This character is a
merchant
.
Sells: Weapons, armor, consumables, crafting ingredients.

quests
.

Hamond's Emporium
", sells a large number of
weapons
,
armor
, consumables, and
crafting ingredients
.
This store is notable in its massive variety of stock. Of all stores, it has the most amount of items on sale by a significant amount.

The Salty Mast
(a brothel in

Hannah Oirakos
is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
. She can be found at the base of the stairs on the lower floor of the
The Charred Barrel
in
Brackenbury
.
Background
As you near, you feel a vibrant history contained in the essence of this woman's soul. Voices from its past seem to call out to you.
You see a woman clad in expensive finery. Her eyes flash as she interrogates a trembling wreck on the ground. Reaching into his pocket, her hand curls around an address, and she smiles grimly. Moments later, she is in a bedraggled alleyway, her pace quickening. Her braids trail around a corner, and she is upon them - or perhaps they are upon her. The action does not last as she easily dispenses of man after man, searching their faces, clothes, shouts for something she fails to find. Frustrated, she moves on, but not quickly enough as an orlan attacks her from behind, the seal upon his finger glittering with malice. The woman's eyes alight upon it as she twists to evade his blow, and her eyes burn bright... perhaps this was not a wasted trip after all.

Harkeen Willthorm
is a character in
Pillars of Eternity
, and a member of
The Forgotten
. He can be found in the center of
Aedelwan Bridge
during
The Forgotten
.
Background
The treasurer of the gang.
As you near, you feel a vibrant history contained in the essence of this man's soul. Voices from its past seem to call out to you.
You see a chamber cloaked in a thick, suffocating darkness. In the center of the room is a dais with some sort of symbol written on the stone. There are six small platforms forming a circle around the dais, all about five feet away from it, each one bearing a similar rune. A man is standing outside the ring, bent over near the base of one of the platforms. He uses a small knife to flake off a little of the stain that was used to paint the symbol.
He stands and walks to the dais, repeating the vandalism on the symbol painted there. He looks around, eyes coming to rest on some powder in an elaborately decorated bowl. He reaches into his robes, pulls out a handful of something that looks like dirt, and drops it into the bowl. He takes an ornamental scepter and stirs the dirt into the powder with it, then wipes the end of the scepter off with the hem of a robe hanging on the wall.
The sound of voices drifts into the room from a closed door on the opposite side of the chamber. Giving one last look around, the man moves quickly toward another exit, stepping out as the other door opens and robed men file in, prepared for their ritual. He hides in the shadows, looking through the curtains drawn across the doorway.
